children.heading: Implementation by Secretary
children.label: a Implementation by Secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall take all actions consistent with law to effectuate the purposes of this chapter.
such State delegates the administration of the State accreditation or certification program to such private entity;
such program is approved by the State; and
such program is consistent with the minimum Federal standards promulgated under this chapter for such program.

children.heading: Noncompliance; proposed legislative changes
children.label: c Noncompliance; proposed legislative changes
children.text: Absent compliance by the States with the provisions of this chapter within three years after , the Secretary shall report to the Congress recommendations for legislative changes considered necessary to assure the States’ compliance with this chapter.August 13, 1981

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 104–66, title I, § 1061(b)109 Stat. 719Dec. 21, 1995
children.label: d Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 104–66, title I, § 1061(b)109 Stat. 719Dec. 21, 1995

children.heading: Existing standards and guidelines
children.label: e Existing standards and guidelines
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, in the case of a State which has, prior to the effective date of standards and guidelines promulgated pursuant to this chapter, established standards for the accreditation of educational programs and certification of radiologic technologists, such State shall be deemed to be in compliance with the conditions of this section unless the Secretary determines, after notice and hearing, that such State standards do not meet the minimum standards prescribed by the Secretary or are inconsistent with the purposes of this chapter.
